question_text: What is the pKa of a sp hybridized carbon atom? A) 25 B) 35 C) 45 D) 50

A) Choose the letter of the molecule with the lowest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). B) Choose the letter of the molecule with the highest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). A) ethane B) propane C) butane D) cyclohexane
Rank the following substituents in order of priority (1 = highest priority): -CH=CH2, -CN, -CH2NH2, -CH2Br

If a chiral molecule has an absolute configuration of R, which direction does it rotate the plane of polarized light? A) clockwise (dextroratory) B) counterclockwise (levarotatory) C) it doesn’t rotate the plane of polarized light D) can’t be determined from the information given

A) Enter the pKa value for each acid in the boxes below. B) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows (a longer arrow is drawn toward the species favored at equilibrium). Shown are two acid-base equilibria: 1) Phenol (C6H5OH) + H2O with two sets of equilibrium arrows between reactants and products (phenolate ion C6H5O- + H3O+), with boxes below phenol and water labeled 8-11 and 15.7 respectively. 2) Acetate ion (CH3COO-) + ammonium ion (CH3NH3+) with two sets of equilibrium arrows between reactants and products (acetic acid CH3COOH + methylamine CH3NH2), with boxes labeled 10-11, 4-5.

A) Provide structures in the boxes to complete the following acid-base (proton-transfer) reactions. B) For each set of reactants, draw in all lone pairs of electrons and show the electron movement by using curved arrows. C) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows. Reaction 1 shows methylamine (CH3NH2) reacting with protonated methylamine (CH3NH3+) and asks to complete two boxes with structures: one protonated methylamine product and one methylamine product. Reaction 2 shows trifluoroacetate ion (CF3COO-) reacting with a proton and has two boxes for structures to complete with curved arrows indicating electron movement and proton transfer. The equilibrium arrows between reactants and products must be chosen for both reactions.
canonical_answer: A) Box 1: protonated methylamine (CH3NH3+ with three bonds to N and 1 positive charge). Box 2: methylamine (CH3NH2). A) Box 3: protonated trifluoroacetic acid (CF3COOH). Box 4: acetate ion (CH3COO-).

question_text: Rank the following sets of molecules in order of acidity (1 = most acidic). First set of molecules with labels chosen: protonated amine with two methyl groups attached to nitrogen (chosen 4), protonated sulfur methyl compound (chosen 2), protonated phosphorus with two methyl groups (chosen 3), protonated chlorine (chosen 1), protonated carbon with three methyl groups attached (chosen 5). Second set of molecules with labels chosen: protonated sulfur compound with methyl attached (chosen 3), protonated sulfur compound with methyl substituent (chosen 1), protonated amino group with methyl substituents (chosen 5), protonated oxygen methyl compound (chosen 2), protonated oxygen compound with hydroxyl substituent (chosen 4).

Met-enkephalin, an endorphin, serves as a natural pain reliever that changes or removes the perception of nerve signals. Label all of the functional groups present in Met-enkephalin. The structure shows a chain with the following labeled functional groups: amine (H2N group), phenol (benzene ring with OH), amide (multiple -CONH- linkages), arene (benzene ring), carboxylic acid (COOH), and sulfide (S-CH3).
canonical_answer: amine; phenol; amide; arene; carboxylic acid; sulfide

question_text: Name the following molecules. 1) An alkane chain with substituents is labeled with carbon numbers 1 to 8 and substituents 3-methyl, 4-isopropyl, 5-methyl, 6-methyl. The combined name given is 4-isopropyl-3,5,6-trimethyloctane. 2) A cycloheptane ring with Br at carbon 1 and a tert-butyl group at carbon 4. The name given is trans-1-bromo-4-tert-butylcycloheptane.

A) Draw the 4 major resonance contributors for the molecule shown in the first box. Partially completed structures are provided as a time-saver. Do not generate any additional charges. B) Draw in all lone pairs of electrons and use arrows to show the movement of electrons within the structure. C) Place a checkmark in the small boxes of the three structures that contribute the most to the resonance hybrid. D) Choose all the nucleophilic atoms in the structure at the bottom of the page (the molecule shows a cyclopentyl ring attached to a ketone, a conjugated alkene, and an N-CH3 group; nucleophilic atoms indicated include the ketone O, the adjacent O, and the nitrogen in the N-CH3 group).
canonical_answer: A) Four drawn resonance contributors with partial structures showing lone pairs and electron movement arrows as in images; B) Lone pairs drawn explicitly on oxygens and nitrogen with curved arrows indicating electron shifts; C) Checkmarks placed on the first, second, and fourth resonance structures; D) Nucleophilic atoms chosen are the ketone oxygen, adjacent oxygen, and nitrogen atom in N-CH3 group circled

question_text: A) Choose the structures that represent a conformation of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along any C-C bond. Four Newman projections are shown: 1) front carbon with Me, Me, H and rear carbon with Me, Me, H; 2) front carbon with Me, Me, H and rear carbon with Et, H, H; 3) front carbon with tBu, H, H and rear carbon with H, H, H; 4) front carbon with Me, Me, H and rear carbon with Me, Me, H. B) Complete each of the Newman projections below to show the most stable and least stable conformations of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along the C2-C3 bond. Two incomplete Newman projections are shown with labels on the front carbon 2: one with groups H3C, H3C, H and the other H3C, H3C, H3C. C) Use the above Newman projections to calculate the barrier to rotation of 2,2-methylbutane sighting along the C2-C3 bond. The barrier to rotation is calculated as 5.4 kcal/mol minus 1.8 kcal/mol equals 3.6 kcal/mol.
canonical_answer: A) Structures 2 and 3 circled. B) Most stable: gauche conformation with 2 CH3 groups staggered; Least stable: eclipsed conformation with methyl groups aligned. C) 3.6 kcal/mol

question_text: A) Draw in the substituents on the ring flipped conformers of each molecule (A and B) to predict which molecule is lower in energy. B) Show the Newman projection for each ring-flipped conformer, sighting along the C5-C6 and C3-C2 bonds. Molecule A and B are 6-membered rings each with three substituents: a methyl group at position 1, a methyl group at position 2, and a chlorine atom at position 3. Several Newman projections and chair conformations are drawn for each. C) Choose the chair conformation that is lower in energy for each molecule. D) Which structure is lower in energy (choose one)? A or B. E) Briefly explain your choice.
canonical_answer: C) Chair conformations of the ring flipped forms indicated by circled Newman projections and chair forms. D) A E) A has fewer 1,3-diaxial interactions in its lowest energy conformation

question_text: A) Label each molecule as chiral (C), achiral (A), or achiral/meso (M). Molecules shown: 1) Bicyclic hydrocarbon with CH3 group labeled A; 2) Compound with NH2, S, Br, OH groups labeled C and chirality centers with R or S; 3) Molecule with two CH3 groups, two OH groups, and two H atoms in a cyclic conformation labeled M with notation 'S-front, R-back'. B) Designate each chirality center as R or S.
canonical_answer: A) A; C; M B) R and S designations shown on second molecule and front S, back R on third molecule

question_text: Indicate the relationships between the two molecules as enantiomers (E), diastereomers (D), or same molecule (S) for each pair: 1) Two pentane diols with OH groups in different stereochemical positions labeled E; 2) Two molecules with Br, CH3, OH, and CH2OH groups in cyclic conformation labeled E; 3) Two molecules with Br, CH3, OH, and ethyl groups, neither cyclic, labeled D.

(4 pts) One of the steps in fat biosynthesis is the hydration of crotonate to yield 3-hydroxybutyrate. The reaction occurs by addition of -OH to the si face at C3, followed by protonation at C2, also from the si face. A) Label the top face of each alkene carbon atom as re or si. B) Show the stereochemistry of the product and C) label C3 as R or S. The crotonate structure shows C3 labeled as re and C2 labeled as si. The product is 3-hydroxybutyrate with OH and H added. The final structure shows the stereochemistry as (S)-3-hydroxybutyrate with OH on the wedge at C3.
canonical_answer: A) C3 labeled re, C2 labeled si; B) Stereochemistry shown as (S)-3-hydroxybutyrate with OH on wedge at C3; C) C3 labeled S
